description: Fix 2 consistently failing E2E tests caused by an ambiguous Playwright locator — button[aria-expanded] matches both the Accordion expand button and the PluginCard options dropdown button, causing strict mode violations.

userStories.title: Fix ambiguous button[aria-expanded] locator in onboarding and side-panel-live-update E2E tests
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want all E2E tests to pass on a clean checkout so that npm run check:everything exits 0.
acceptanceCriteria: e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts test 'adding a plugin transitions the side panel from no-plugins to plugin list' passes
e2e/side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts test 'side panel updates plugin list when config.json adds a plugin' passes
The locator used to verify plugin cards is specific enough to match exactly one element (e.g., the Accordion trigger or a data-testid), avoiding Playwright strict mode violations
All other existing E2E tests continue to pass (no regressions)
userStories.notes: Both tests fail with identical root cause: `sidePanelPage.locator('button[aria-expanded]')` resolves to 2 elements — (1) the Radix Accordion trigger button (with `data-state='closed'`, `data-orientation='vertical'`, `data-radix-collection-item`) and (2) the PluginCard options dropdown button (with `aria-haspopup='menu'`, `aria-label='Plugin options'`). The fix is to make the locator more specific. Options: use `sidePanelPage.locator('[data-radix-collection-item]')` to target just the Accordion trigger, or use `sidePanelPage.getByRole('button', { name: /E2E Test/ })` to target by accessible name, or use `sidePanelPage.locator('text=E2E Test')` which is already asserted on the next line (so the `button[aria-expanded]` assertion may be redundant — consider replacing it with a check for the plugin card container instead). Files: `e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts:120` and `e2e/side-panel-live-update.e2e.ts:93` — both have the exact same line: `await expect(sidePanelPage.locator('button[aria-expanded]')).toBeVisible({ timeout: 10_000 });`
